omitted.text: The solution should send alerts (e.g., email, SMS) to the user if the user chooses to whenever any action has been taken on the alert.
The solution should enable the help-desk user to view the reports on the submitted defects or enhancement requests category-wise, status-wise, and age- wise.
The support solution should be accessible to the users both from within the application and also outside the application through a browser interface.
it may be subject to re-department and copying to removable media if required, so long as its contents remain unchanged.
The System must at a minimum be able to provide reports for actions on cases organised: By case;
The System should be able to provide reports for actions on cases organised by workstation and (where technically appropriate) by network address.
the most stringent) implies that the System must not include such cases in any count of search results;
If a user performs a quick or advanced search, the System must never include in the search result list any record which the user does not have the right to access.
Such configurations must be saved in the user profile.

Providing text equivalents for non-text media objects: All non-text media objects, such as graphical images or video, should be provided with alternative equivalent textual descriptions and/or with equivalent text-based functionality.
Making navigation self-descriptive: Navigation should be designed to help users understand where they are, where they have been and where they can go next.

Acceptable opening / download times: Application pages should be designed and implemented so that there are acceptable opening times and download times for the expected range of technical contexts of use (e.g.

hallucinated.text: The system must allow the user to track logged enhancement requests on the application thereafter.
The system must avoid requiring users to click through endless screens to complete their daily work.
The navigation component must clearly indicate where the user is currently located.

The system must be designed in a manner that operational data is not lost in case of any failure of equipment or communication network, and it must hold onto that information safely until the connection is back.
If a user requests access to, or searches for, a case which he does not have the right to access, the exact response needs to be selectable at configuration time.
The audit trail data cannot be deleted by any user.
Whenever possible, error messages must spell out the actions the user can take to resolve the error, rather than just giving them a dead end.
The system must support police operations that run around the clock.
System restoration following a software or hardware failure must be complete with inline synchronization.
The system must provide solid role-based control for the functionality within the system to manage permissions.
The system design must take into account human capabilities and restrictions in perceiving color.
The police will take the complaint forward based on the evidence, facts and following investigation.
